name: OWASP Top 10 Protection
description: Core WAF rules covering the OWASP Top 10 web application security risks. Detects SQL injection, cross-site scripting (XSS), path traversal, remote code execution, and server-side request forgery. Recommended as a baseline for all web applications.
author: Ghostwire Proxy

rules.name: SQL Injection — Union/Select
rules.description: Detects common SQL injection patterns including UNION SELECT, INSERT INTO, DROP TABLE.

rules.name: SQL Injection — Boolean/Error Based
rules.description: Detects boolean-based and error-based SQL injection techniques.

rules.name: SQL Injection — Comment Evasion
rules.description: Detects SQL comment sequences used to evade filters.

rules.name: XSS — Script Tags
rules.description: Detects script tags and inline JavaScript event handlers.

rules.name: XSS — Encoded Payloads
rules.description: Detects HTML entity encoded and URL encoded XSS payloads.

rules.name: Path Traversal
rules.description: Detects directory traversal attempts to access files outside the web root.

rules.name: Remote Code Execution — Command Injection
rules.description: Detects OS command injection via semicolons, pipes, backticks, and command substitution.

rules.name: Remote Code Execution — PHP/Java
rules.description: Detects PHP stream wrappers and function calls used for remote code execution.

rules.name: SSRF — Internal Network Access
rules.description: Detects server-side request forgery attempts targeting internal/private networks and cloud metadata.
